title: ConfigurationColumn Object
description: Represents a column in a configuration table. This is the base class for the more specific column types. The "Name" column is not considered a standard column but is a value associated with each table row.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: Deletes this column from the table. Property columns cannot be deleted. If the table was obtained from a DataFile, this method will fail.

properties.name: id
properties.description: The id of the column. This is constant and cannot be changed by the user.

properties.name: index
properties.description: The index position of this column within the table. The first column is at index 0 and does not include the "Name" column.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: rowCount
properties.description: Returns the number of rows in this column.

properties.name: title
properties.description: The title of this column. In a top table, this can only be edited for suppression, visibility, parameter, and theme table columns. It behaves as read-only for all other types. In a theme table, the title of all the columns can be modified except for the column that represents the root component for materials and appearances.   If the table was obtained from a DataFile, this property behaves as read-only for all the columns.

classType.title: ConfigurationColumn.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

deleteMe.title: ConfigurationColumn.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.description: Deletes this column from the table. Property columns cannot be deleted. If the table was obtained from a DataFile, this method will fail.
deleteMe.return_description: Returns true if the deletion was successful.

id.title: ConfigurationColumn.id Property
id.description: The id of the column. This is constant and cannot be changed by the user.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = configurationColumn_var.id

title.title: ConfigurationColumn.title Property
title.description: The title of this column. In a top table, this can only be edited for suppression, visibility, parameter, and theme table columns. It behaves as read-only for all other types. In a theme table, the title of all the columns can be modified except for the column that represents the root component for materials and appearances. If the table was obtained from a DataFile, this property behaves as read-only for all the columns.

syntax.python:

propertyValue = configurationColumn_var.title

# Set the value of the property.
configurationColumn_var.title = propertyValue
